content.problemStatement:
You have initially a string of **N** characters, denoted by **A1,A2...AN**. You have to print the size of the largest subsequence of string **A** such that all the characters in that subsequence are distinct ie. no two characters in that subsequence should be same.

A subsequence of string **A** is a sequence that can be derived from **A** by deleting some elements  and without changing the order of the remaining elements.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a non-empty string S consisting of printable ASCII characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the size of the largest subsequence of string S such that all the characters in that subsequence are distinct.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All three characters are different, so the whole string can be taken as the subsequence.
The character 'a' appears twice; we can keep one 'a' and the 'b', giving a subsequence of length 2.
All characters are distinct, therefore the answer equals the string length.

solution.approach: The key insight is to use a boolean array or a set to keep track of the characters we have seen so far. We iterate through the string and for each character, we check if it has been seen before. If not, we increment the count of distinct characters.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum length of the string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) as we are using a fixed-size boolean array of size 256 or a set.
